title: Queries where a "is null" clause is combined with two equals clauses fail to produce the expected result.
poc:
CREATE TABLE "sys"."c__has_a" (
         "owner_table"    VARCHAR(32672),
         "owner_id"       BIGINT,
         "relation_name"  VARCHAR(32672),
         "property_table" VARCHAR(32672),
         "property_id"    BIGINT,
         "property_class" VARCHAR(32672)
 );

CREATE TABLE FOO (
        "a" VARCHAR(8),
        "b" VARCHAR(8),
        "c" BIGINT);
select * from c__has_a where owner_table is null and property_table='SIMPLE';
key_bug_features: is null combined with two equals clauses
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE sys.c__has_a (owner_table VARCHAR(32672), owner_id BIGINT, relation_name VARCHAR(32672), property_table VARCHAR(32672), property_id BIGINT, property_class VARCHAR(32672));
CREATE TABLE FOO (a VARCHAR(8), b VARCHAR(8), c BIGINT);
INSERT INTO sys.c__has_a (owner_table, owner_id, relation_name, property_table, property_id, property_class) VALUES (NULL, 1, 'relation1', 'SIMPLE', 100, 'class1');
INSERT INTO FOO (a, b, c) VALUES ('val1', 'val2', NULL);
SELECT * FROM sys.c__has_a WHERE owner_table IS NULL AND property_table = 'SIMPLE' AND property_id = 100;
